title: Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple
tagline: Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Piramanoor Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple is an ancient Shiva temple(now under renovation) in Piramanoor, a village in Tiruppuvanam Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Piramanoor Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple is an ancient Shiva temple(now under renovation) in Piramanoor, a village in Tiruppuvanam Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India. Towns Near By Tiruppuvanam (5 km), Madurai (23 km), Sivaganga (24.3 km), Manamadurai(25.2 km). Other villages in Tiruppuvanam Taluk are Tiruppuvanam, Achangulam, Allinagaram, Chellapanendal, Ilandaikulam, K.Pethanendal Reason for the Name Piramanoor: Lord Bramha came here to worship the Linga brought by him from the Kailasa Mountain. As per his request, the place is called as Piramanoor(Brahman+Oor).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Madapuram
topPlaces.description: Madurapuram is a small village in India that lies on the banks of the Vaigai River and on the National Highway 49 that connects Madurai with Rameshwaram. The village is situated 18 kilometres (11 mi) from Madurai.

topPlaces.name: Pushpavaneswarar temple
topPlaces.description: Pushpavaneswarar temple (also called Poovananathar temple or Thirpoovanam temple) in Thirupuvanam, a town in Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 7th century.

topPlaces.name: Thirupuvanam, Sivaganga
topPlaces.description: Thiruppuvanam is a town in Manamadurai Assembly constituency and also Manamadurai division in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

items.note: Madurapuram is a small village in India that lies on the banks of the Vaigai River and on the National Highway 49 that connects Madurai with Rameshwaram. The village is situated 18 kilometres (11 mi) from Madurai.

Pushpavaneswarar temple (also called Poovananathar temple or Thirpoovanam temple) in Thirupuvanam, a town in Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 7th century.
Thiruppuvanam is a town in Manamadurai Assembly constituency and also Manamadurai division in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (24 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Piramanoor Kailasha Nathar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Madapuram, Pushpavaneswarar temple, Thirupuvanam, Sivaganga.
